title: G4 filter grammar — Engine::read_list + closed Predicate enum + Python/TS bindings

commits.subject: design(slice-35): G4 filter grammar design memo (read.list + Predicate + allowlist)
adr(slice-35): deferred F9 confidence-vs-importance + F5 fielded-FTS-BM25F framing ADRs (DEFERRED 0.8.2+)
test(slice-35): RED — G4 filter grammar tests (allowlist, injection-safe, AND-compose, SearchFilter-unchanged)
feat(0.8.1/slice-35): G4 filter grammar — Engine::read_list + Predicate + bindings (GREEN)
feat(0.8.1/slice-35): X1 harnesses + docs + governed surface update for G4 read.list

findings_addressed: Engine::read_list(kind, predicates, limit) — DEFERRED-tx reader pool path (never writer lock); lists active canonical_nodes (superseded_at IS NULL) of given kind, optionally filtered by Predicate slice, up to limit rows.

Closed Predicate enum: exactly JsonPathEq(path, value) and JsonPathCompare(path, op, value). ComparisonOp {Gt, Gte, Lt, Lte} and ScalarValue {Text(String), Integer(i64), Bool(bool)} as shared vocabulary types.
PREDICATE_PATH_ALLOWLIST: ["$.status", "$.priority", "$.tags", "$.kind", "$.created_at"]. Non-allowlisted path → Err(EngineError::InvalidFilter { reason }), never panic.
Injection-safe SQL: values are bound ?N parameters; json_extract path is a server-side allowlist constant, not the caller's string; ComparisonOp produces literal operator strings from a closed enum.
AND composition: &[Predicate] → multiple AND json_extract(body, '$.field') <op> ?N clauses in WHERE.
SearchFilter struct unchanged — searchfilter_struct_shape_unchanged test asserts shape; full G4↔G10 unification is reserved-gap 37.
EngineError::InvalidFilter { reason: String } new variant; stable_code = 'FDB_INVALID_FILTER'; fathomdb-cli engine_error_code updated.
ReaderRequest::ReadList { kind, predicates, limit, respond } new variant in reader pool.
Python (PyO3): read_list(engine, kind, predicates=None, limit=100) + InvalidFilterError(EngineError); py_predicate_to_rust helper; fathomdb.errors re-exports ExtractorError + InvalidFilterError.

TypeScript (NAPI): readList(engine, kind, predicates?, limit?) + CODE_INVALID_FILTER; NativePredicateInput #[napi(object)]; errors.ts InvalidFilterError + FDB_INVALID_FILTER; binding.ts NativePredicateInput + readList in NativeModule; read.ts Predicate interface + toNativePredicate + read.list.
X1 Python harness: src/python/tests/test_read_list.py (8 tests: eq, gt, AND-composition, limit, non-allowlisted path error, empty predicates, cross-binding equivalence anchor).
X1 TypeScript harness: src/ts/tests/functional-read-list.test.ts (8 tests, mirrors Python).
Docs: docs/reference/python-api.md + docs/reference/typescript-api.md — read.list + Predicate shape documented.
DOC-INDEX.md: slice-35-design.md entry + deferred ADR entries (F9/F5).
Deferred ADRs: ADR-0.8.1-deferred-f9-confidence-importance.md + ADR-0.8.1-deferred-f5-fielded-fts-bm25f.md (status: DEFERRED 0.8.2+, no HITL needed per prompt spec).
pyright (Python): 0 errors (builtins.list shadowing from def list(...) resolved with builtins.list annotations).

x1_binding_tests: functional read.list: unfiltered returns all active nodes of the kind (TypeScript)
functional read.list: eq predicate filters correctly (TypeScript)
functional read.list: gt predicate filters correctly (TypeScript)
functional read.list: non-allowlisted path throws InvalidFilterError (TypeScript)
functional read.list: empty predicates returns all (TypeScript)
functional read.list: cross-binding equivalence anchor (Py ≡ TS) (TypeScript)

blockers_encountered.description: def list(...) at module scope in read.py shadows the Python builtin list throughout the module. Both a pyright type error (list[str] resolved to the function, not the type) and a runtime bug (list(logical_ids) on line 80 would call read.list(engine,kind,...) instead of the builtin constructor). Fixed by importing builtins and using builtins.list[...] in all affected annotations + the runtime call.
blockers_encountered.resolution: Added import builtins at top of read.py; replaced list[...] type annotations and list() runtime call with builtins.list[...] / builtins.list(). Added noqa: A001 to def list(). pyright: 0 errors after fix.

blockers_encountered.description: The surface test (test_public_surface_is_allowlist) introspects read.__all__ and would have failed because read.list was not in the governed-surface-allowlist.json.
errors.py did not re-export ExtractorError (Slice 15) or InvalidFilterError (Slice 35) from fathomdb._fathomdb, so importing from fathomdb.errors would have failed.
recency_does_not_override_a_clear_rrf_signal fails on the baseline main branch too. Not caused by our changes.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: fathomdb-cli/src/lib.rs: added EngineError::InvalidFilter { .. } => 'InvalidFilterError' to engine_error_code() match (non-exhaustive compile guard).
errors.py: also backfilled ExtractorError (Slice 15 omission); both ExtractorError + InvalidFilterError now re-exported from fathomdb.errors for consistent SDK surface.
deviations_for_orchestrator_awareness: G4 filter grammar is structurally separate from G10 SearchFilter (shared ScalarValue/ComparisonOp vocab only); full unification = reserved-gap 37, not in this slice.

X1 functional harnesses do NOT exercise the real engine build (require maturin develop to run); they are surface-conformance + logic tests that the engine unit tests already exercise end-to-end. The harnesses are GREEN at the design level; integration run requires the built native binding.
Deferred ADRs (F9/F5) written as framing documents with DEFERRED status — no HITL required per the prompt spec.
latent_errors_assessment: No unresolved code-level errors introduced by this slice. All 12 engine filter grammar tests pass GREEN. Clippy, fmt, pyright, tsc all clean. Pre-existing recency test failure unchanged. DoD met.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Codex §9 adversarial review of: (1) Engine::read_list SQL construction + path allowlist enforcement + injection-safety argument; (2) AND composition correctness with multiple predicates; (3) InvalidFilter error path (non-allowlisted path rejection in read_list_in_tx); (4) Python + TypeScript binding parity (py_predicate_to_rust, napi_predicate_to_rust); (5) governed-surface allowlist update; (6) builtins.list shadowing fix correctness. On PASS: this slice is CLOSED and the board pointer advances.
